steps.description:
That's a mistake probably on my part :)

In this use case, the upstream target is changing rapidly. scaling up and down, or containers are replaced quickly with a new one (with a new IP). And service discovery is available using the internal dns server.

Using this DNS way, we would avoid

Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "proxy: Load Balance using multiple A records per single…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
That's a mistake probably on my part :)

In this use case, the upstream target is changing rapidly. scaling up and down, or containers are replaced quickly with a new one (with a new IP). And service discovery is available using the internal dns server.

Using this DNS way, we would avoid generating web server configuration using a template file, and restarting/reloading it on each change etc.
codeSnippets:
It'd return 502 until any host added to upstream via API.
